chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الصَّلَاةِ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ أَهْلِ بَيْتِهِ عَلَيْهِمُ السَّلَامُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 20 – The Salawāt (Blessings) upon the Prophet Muhammad<sup>saww</sup> and the People<sup>asws</sup> of his<sup>saww</sup> Household

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/86)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/86)
text: لَا يَزَالُ الدُّعَاءُ مَحْجُوباً حَتَّى يُصَلَّى عَلَى مُحَمَّدٍ وَ آلِ مُحَمَّدٍ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hisham Bin Salim,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws having said: ‘A supplication remains barred until he (the one who is supplicating) sends Salawāt upon Muhammadsaww and the Progenyasws of Muhammadsaww’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hisham ibn Salim from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “A prayer remains barred until one asks favors (in the form of al-Salat (the special expression called al-Salat ‘ Ala al-Nabiyy) from Allah for Muhammad and his family, recipient of divine supreme covenant.”
